idea: cuBLAS reference baseline
1p2c split-output WN=128 STAGES=2 non-persistent fp16->fp32; first functional baseline at 198.6 TFLOPS
expected_gain: establish baseline, expect 300+ TFLOPS with optimization

idea: Switched from 1p2c to 1p1c WN=256 STAGES=3: reduced CTA threads from 384 to 256, enabling 2+ CTAs/SM vs prior 1 CTA/SM at 128 regs/thread
Persistent 1p1c WN=256 STAGES=4 with 114 CTAs: only 114 parallel CTAs serializes 2352 tiles each per CTA, causing massive execution serialization vs non-persistent 16384 CTAs
1p1c WN=256 STAGES=3 maxrregcount=160: same as iter002 at 291 TFLOPS, register spilling has no benefit
1p1c WN=256 STAGES=3 padded output SMEM: same result 292T, padding did not reduce bank conflicts since Choreo already handles layout. Root issue is SMEM at 184KB prevents 2 CTAs/SM
1p1c WN=128 STAGES=3 hoped for 2 CTAs/SM via SMEM reduction: got worse at 206T vs 291T at WN=256. Halving WN halves arithmetic intensity per CTA, losing more than we gain from extra CTAs
1p1c WN=192 STAGES=2: 196T, even worse than WN=256. Narrower tile consistently loses. Must stay at WN=256 and fix latency differently
1p1c WN=256 STAGES=4: increased stages from 3 to 4, slight improvement 291T->293T. More prefetch depth slightly reduces CTA barrier wait. SMEM=224KB barely fits in 228KB
1p2c split-output WN=256 TM=128 STAGES=2 FP32: verification fails with all-zero output, suspected Choreo codegen issue with 2 consumer warpgroups and FP32 TMA output SMEM size 64KB box

1p3c WN=128 TM=192 STAGES=3: verified correct but 218.7T vs iter008 293.3T; 512-thread block requires maxrregcount=128 causing spilling; lower WN=128 reduces arithmetic intensity; structural change confirmed: sync tma.copy works for output but registers are the bottleneck
1p1c WN=128 STAGES=3 for 2 CTAs/SM: 104KB SMEM. Lower AI hurt more than occupancy gain.
1p1c WN=128 STAGES=3 for 2 CTAs/SM: 104KB SMEM. Lower AI hurt more than occupancy gain.
WN=256 STAGES=4 1p1c + --use-warpspec --hoist-offset: explicit warpgroup barriers + address hoist
NCU R12: same metrics as iter008. Barrier stall 3.75 (TMA 3x slower than WGMMA). Next: manual CU optimization to overlap WGMMA with TMA refill.
tma-cluster-aware: uses cluster TMA mbarrier. No cluster launch config -> cluster_size=1. Less efficient than standard cp.async.bulk.
cluster TMA (tma-cluster-aware) without cluster_size launch config: cluster_size=1 degrades vs standard cp.async.bulk
Manual .cu: L2_PROMOTION_L2_256B for both lhs and rhs TMA tensor maps. B tile 32KB stays in L2 across multiple CTAs reading same N-strip.
NCU R14: L2 promotion worked - LTS 68->75%, DRAM 60.9->60.3%. Barrier still 3.75. Next: maxrregcount=256 and cluster TMA.
warpgroup_arrive() moved before iv_warp loop + L2_256B. No measurable improvement. warpgroup_arrive() position doesnt matter.
maxrregcount=256 ptxas ignores it - unconstrained regs cause spilling. Worse 147T vs 299T.
maxrregcount=256 ignored by ptxas: unconstrained regs -> severe spilling, 147T
2x K-loop unroll: process 2 K-tiles per iteration, issue both WGMMA batches before waiting. Overlaps tensor core pipeline. +12T vs iter014.
2x K-unroll: 311.8T. Next: try depth-2 WGMMA pipeline with warpgroup_wait<1>.
4x K-unroll: waits for 4 stages before computing, causing 4x initial stall. 172T.
Depth-2 WGMMA pipeline: prime WGMMA[0], then each iter waits full[k] while WGMMA[k-1] computes, drains k-1, signals empty, issues WGMMA[k]. Barrier stall 3.75->1.61, TC 59->68%.
depth2 WGMMA pipeline with wait<1>: issue WGMMA[k] before WGMMA[k-1] drains, use warpgroup_wait<1> to keep 2 batches in flight
WARP_N=192, STAGES=5: smaller N-tile to allow 5 pipeline stages, applying iter019 depth-1 WGMMA overlap; hypothesis: more prefetch stages hides TMA latency better
TILE_K=128 STAGES=2: larger K-tile to reduce TMA transactions and barrier overhead, depth-1 WGMMA overlap
1p2c FP32 TILE_M=128 WARP_N=128 STAGES=3: two consumer WGs share B-tile to reduce DRAM
1p2c reference FP32 adaptation (wn160, no depth-1 pipeline) — barrier stalls 3.96 vs 1.61 in best; no WGMMA-TMA overlap kills perf. Pipeline overlap is essential.
1p2c+depth1+STAGES=3: extend stage count from 2 to 3 for more pipeline depth. Failed numerically — SMEM swizzle layout for 3 stages breaks correctness.
1p2c+depth1+STAGES=2: depth-1 WGMMA pipeline in both 1p2c consumers. 207.5T vs 178.9T without pipeline. But WARP_N=160 too small vs iter019 WARP_N=256. Need larger N-tile.

1p2c WARP_N=256 STAGES=2: producer loads 128x64 A + 256x64 B; two consumers share B, each gets half of A rows. Built successfully but numerically incorrect. Consumer2 +4096 offset or barrier race issue unclear.
Iter019 with maxrregcount=192: 320T vs 321.8T at mreg=160. Extra registers did not help. mreg=160 remains optimal for WARP_N=256.
Iter019 with -Xptxas -O3: PTXAS optimizer level 3 generates better instruction scheduling. +3.5T gain (321.8->325.3T). Simple compiler flag improvement on best kernel.
Grid swizzle SWIZZLE=16: 16 M-tiles per super-tile for better B-matrix L2 reuse, improving L2 hit rate and tensor core utilization
Grid swizzle SWIZZLE=32: 32 M-tiles per super-tile. Slightly worse than SWIZZLE=16 (362.8T)
Grid swizzle SWIZZLE=64: full M-column super-tiles. Worse than SWIZZLE=16 (362.8T). SWIZZLE=16 is the sweet spot.
SWIZZLE=16 + L2 cudaStreamAttributeAccessPolicyWindow on B-matrix (persisting). B=512MB >> L2=50MB, so minimal additional benefit over swizzle alone.
SWIZZLE=16 + maxrregcount=192 (vs 160 in iter034). Higher register limit allows better warp scheduling, consistently ~4T faster than iter034 at 362.8T baseline.
SWIZZLE=16 + maxrregcount=192 vs 160 in iter034. Higher register limit, consistently ~4T faster.
SWIZZLE=16 no maxrregcount limit. More registers per warp reduces occupancy, worse than maxrregcount=160 (362.8T).
SWIZZLE=16 maxrregcount=224: too many regs per warp, kills occupancy, 268T vs 362.8T baseline.
SWIZZLE=16 + WGMMA depth-2 pipeline (wait<1>, 2 WGMMAs in-flight). More register pressure + complex barrier logic, worse 342.6T vs 362.8T.
2D swizzle SWIZZLE_M=16 SWIZZLE_N=4: 64-CTA super-tiles for both A and B reuse. Worse 357T vs 362.8T. 1D N-major swizzle is better.
SWIZZLE=16 with --use-warpspec compiler flag. No improvement over iter034 (362.8T). Manual warpspec already implemented.
SWIZZLE=16 K-unroll=2: process 2 K-tiles per iteration. More barrier overhead, worse 334.7T vs 362.8T.
SWIZZLE=16 STAGES=3: smaller SMEM 184KB allows 2CTAs/SM. But insufficient prefetch depth hurts: 348.5T vs 362.8T.
SWIZZLE=16 serpentine: odd super-tiles reverse N order. Marginal benefit over iter034 (362.8T), essentially same performance at ~360T.
Pure N-major (column-major) order, no super-tiles. Too many concurrent M-tiles sharing same N-strip floods L2, 325.3T. SWIZZLE=16 sweet spot confirmed.
SWIZZLE=16 same source as iter034. No change, baseline confirmation at 360T.
WARP_N=192 STAGES=5 SWIZZLE=16. Lower arithmetic intensity per CTA than WARP_N=256, 294.3T vs 362.8T.
SWIZZLE=16 without DMA_DIAGNOSIS flag. Same performance as iter034 - debug flags not affecting perf.
2-CTA cluster TMA multicast for B-matrix. Kernel crashes with CUDA error 719 (launch failure) - cluster barrier implementation incorrect.
SWIZZLE=16 with -Xptxas -dlcm=cg (global load cache mode). No improvement, TMA bypasses L1 already.
1p2c TILE_M=128 3WG: CUDA 719 - barrier count mismatch. Complex 1p2c barrier logic. Discard.
1P2C warp specialization: 2 consumers (top/bottom 64-row halves of 128-row M-tile), WARP_N=256, FP32 accumulators (mc[128]), SWIZZLE=16 grid, maxrregcount=160 for 384-thread launch feasibility
WGMMA depth-2 pipeline to hide latency; dual accumulator sets increase register demand
TILE_K=32 STAGES=4 to get deeper pipeline depth with same SMEM; smaller K-tile increases barrier overhead
SWIZZLE=8 grid ordering to improve L2 locality for 114 SM layout; slightly worse than SWIZZLE=16
SWIZZLE=32 grid ordering; worse than SWIZZLE=16 for this matrix size
1P1C WN=128 for 2 blocks/SM occupancy; WGMMA serialized due to 128 fp32 accumulators for top+bottom halves exceeding register budget
Manual unroll of iv_warp=0..3 loop in both consumers; removes loop overhead and helps compiler schedule WGMMA better
pragma unroll 2 on K-loop + manual iv_warp unroll; halves K-loop branch overhead and enables better WGMMA scheduling; BEATS cuBLAS 421.6T
pragma unroll 64 on K-loop; optimal unroll factor for 256 K-tiles; reduces loop overhead and enables PTX to eliminate barriers more aggressively
L2 persistence for rhs B-matrix; overhead of setup outweighs benefit since B is too large for L2
warpgroup_wait<1> depth-1 WGMMA pipeline + K-loop unroll=64; hides full[stage].wait barrier latency inside WGMMA execution; 460T = 109% cuBLAS
warpgroup_wait<2> depth-2 WGMMA pipeline + K-loop unroll=64; 2 WGMMA groups in-flight hides barrier latency better; 478.6T = 113.5% cuBLAS
wait<1> + single warpgroup_arrive per K-tile + unroll=64; fusing 4 WGMMA into single group with 1 arrive; 486T = 115.2% cuBLAS
front-loaded warpgroup_wait before full.wait; worse than iter069 486T
precomputed SMEM descriptors for stage 0 and 1 to avoid recomputation in K-loop; no improvement vs iter069 - compiler already optimizes this with unroll=64
